## Summary

- what changed
- why it changed

## Verification

- commands run
- results

## Public Surface Check

- [ ] support wording still points to the public issue tracker
- [ ] security wording still points to `SECURITY.md`
- [ ] no logs, caches, runtime artifacts, or agent working directories were added to the tracked surface
- [ ] README, landing page, manifest description, and front-door wording still agree when public-facing copy changed
- [ ] `CHANGELOG.md` still matches the tracked release story when release-facing copy, release assets, or governance wording changed
- [ ] GitHub release narrative is treated as a mirror of `CHANGELOG.md`, and the latest release page was manually checked when preparing a tag
- [ ] tracked screenshots, GIF, and social preview were regenerated or intentionally left unchanged when UI-facing visuals changed
- [ ] GitHub custom Social Preview remains enabled, and the live image was manually checked when front-door visuals changed
- [ ] Discussions, Releases, and Pages links still point to the expected public surface when front-door links changed

## Runtime Impact

- [ ] no user-facing behavior changed
- [ ] supported site behavior changed and is explained above
- [ ] permissions or public identity changed and are explained above
